stories.headline: FOMC decision lands tomorrow with BTC pinned at $77,794 and bid thinning
stories.tldr: Bitcoin holds $77,794 into the April 30 FOMC after stalling 2% below Wednesday's $79,388 high as ether, XRP and SOL all close red.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A hawkish surprise lands on a thin order book — spot volume just hit the lowest since October 2023 — so any Powell pivot or tape-bomb gets amplified into outsized moves.
the_details: BTC trades $77,794 on April 29, +0.4% over 24h but $1,594 off Wednesday's intraday high.
FOMC announces rates April 30 — third meeting of 2026 and first since the April ETF inflow wave.
ETH, XRP and SOL all closed in the red, signaling risk-off positioning ahead of the print.

stories.headline: Strategy's STRC swallowed 77,000 BTC YTD — 9.6x every US spot ETF combined
stories.tldr: Saylor's STRC vehicle has hoovered roughly 77,000 BTC in 2026 versus just 8,000 BTC of net inflows across every US spot Bitcoin ETF combined, per River.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: One balance-sheet buyer is now the marginal price-setter for Bitcoin — not BlackRock, not Fidelity — concentrating supply risk in a single Nasdaq-listed equity story.
the_details: STRC pulled in 77,000 BTC YTD, dwarfing the 8,000 BTC net for all US spot BTC ETFs combined.
MSTR stock jumped 37% after April's $2.54B BTC purchase announcement, per Bitcoin Magazine.
River projects Strategy could cross 1 million BTC of holdings by late 2026 if pace holds.

stories.headline: SEC-CFTC joint guidance ratifies BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, LINK as digital commodities
stories.tldr: The SEC and CFTC inked a joint interpretation declaring most digital assets non-securities and naming BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP and LINK as digital commodities.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The two-year jurisdictional standoff is over on paper — exchanges can list these five without an SEC registration overhang, unlocking institutional desks that were sidelined on legal risk.
the_details: Joint interpretation creates a five-bucket taxonomy: digital commodities, collectibles, tools, stablecoins, securities.
BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, LINK explicitly named as digital commodities under CFTC oversight.
Lands amid GENIUS Act stablecoin rulemakings — FDIC (April 7), Treasury (April 3), FinCEN/OFAC (April 8).

stories.headline: DeFi United closes 100,000 ETH war-chest to backstop rsETH after $292M KelpDAO drain
stories.tldr: A DeFi United coalition raised over 100,000 ETH to restore rsETH backing after the April 18 LayerZero-vector exploit drained roughly $292 million from KelpDAO.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Community recapitalization — not a Tether-style bailout — sets the new norm for cross-chain LRT failures, but it locks restakers into multi-month redemption queues to absorb the loss.
the_details: 100,000+ ETH committed to make rsETH holders whole after the April 18 bridge exploit.
KelpDAO breach drained ~$292M, the largest DeFi exploit of 2026 and 95% of April losses with Drift.
Jefferies warns the marquee hacks could chill Wall Street's near-term DeFi tokenization appetite.

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan turn Consensus Miami sponsors as TradFi crashes May 5-7
stories.tldr: Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan are sponsoring — not just speaking at — Consensus Miami May 5-7, headlining the most institutional-heavy lineup the conference has hosted.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Bulge-bracket sponsorships convert crypto from satellite to core line item in TradFi marketing budgets — a structural buy signal even if BTC chops sideways into the event.
the_details: Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan listed as sponsors, not just speakers, per CoinDesk reporting.
Conference dates May 5-7 in Miami with federal policymakers and crypto pioneers on bill.
Marks first time both bulge-brackets have committed sponsorship dollars to a Consensus event.
